title: Power Platform Environment
description: Schema for a Microsoft Power Platform environment resource as returned by the Power Platform Admin API (BAP endpoint) and the Power Platform Unified API. An environment is a container that stores, manages, and shares business data, apps, chatbots, and flows. Each tenant has a default environment shared by all users, and administrators can create additional production, sandbox, trial, and developer environments.

id.description: The fully qualified resource ID of the environment in Azure Resource Manager format.

type.description: The Azure resource type for the environment.

location.description: The geographic location (geo) where the environment is deployed. Determines data residency and available features.

name.description: The environment identifier (GUID). This serves as the primary key for addressing the environment in API calls.

properties.description: Detailed properties of the environment including configuration, status, capacity, and linked service metadata.

azureRegion.description: The specific Azure region within the geo where the environment is hosted.

displayName.description: The human-readable display name of the environment as shown in the Power Platform admin center.

description.description: An optional text description of the environment purpose.
examples: This is my environment purpose description

createdTime.description: The UTC timestamp when the environment was created.

lastModifiedTime.description: The UTC timestamp of the last modification to the environment.

provisioningState.description: The current provisioning state of the environment.

creationType.description: Indicates how the environment was created.

environmentSku.description: The SKU type of the environment, which determines its capabilities, licensing requirements, and lifecycle. Production environments require paid licenses and at least 1 GB of database capacity. Sandbox environments can be reset. Trial environments expire after 30 days. Developer environments are for individual development use.

isDefault.description: Whether this is the default environment for the tenant. Each tenant has exactly one default environment that is shared by all users.

capacity.description: Capacity consumption details for the environment, broken down by database, file, and log storage.

addons.description: Add-on allocations for the environment such as app passes, per-flow plans, portal views, and AI credits.

clientUris.description: URLs for accessing the environment through different client interfaces.

admin.description: URL to the Power Platform admin center hub page for this environment.

maker.description: URL to the Power Apps maker portal home page for this environment.

runtimeEndpoints.description: Regional runtime service endpoint URLs for each Power Platform service in this environment.

microsoft.BusinessAppPlatform.description: The Business Application Platform (BAP) API endpoint.

microsoft.CommonDataModel.description: The Common Data Service (Dataverse) API endpoint.

microsoft.PowerAppsAdvisor.description: The Power Apps Advisor API endpoint.

microsoft.ApiManagement.description: The API Management endpoint for connectors.

databaseType.description: The type of database provisioned for the environment. CommonDataService indicates a Dataverse database is present.

notificationMetadata.description: Notification configuration metadata for the environment.

retentionPeriod.description: The data retention period in ISO 8601 duration format.

states.description: Current management and runtime states of the environment.

management.description: The management state indicates whether the environment is ready for administrative operations.

runtime.description: The runtime state indicates whether the environment is available for app and flow execution.

updateCadence.description: The update cadence setting controlling how frequently the environment receives new features and updates.

id.description: Frequent provides updates multiple times per month. Moderate provides updates at least once per month.

retentionPeriod.description: The backup retention period in ISO 8601 duration format.

backupsAvailableFromDateTime.description: The earliest timestamp from which backups are available.

protectionStatus.description: Encryption key management information for the environment.

keyManagedBy.description: Whether the encryption key is managed by Microsoft or by the customer (customer-managed keys / CMK).

cluster.description: Information about the infrastructure cluster hosting the environment.

connectedGroups.description: List of Microsoft Entra ID groups connected to the environment for access control.

Principal.description: Represents a principal (user, service principal, or delegated admin) that performed an action on the environment.

id.description: The unique identifier (object ID) of the principal in Microsoft Entra ID.

displayName.description: The display name of the principal.

tenantId.description: The Microsoft Entra tenant ID of the principal.

CapacityInfo.description: Capacity consumption information for a specific storage type within the environment.

capacityType.description: The type of capacity. Database is Dataverse database storage, File is file/attachment storage, Log is audit log storage.

capacityUnit.description: The unit of measurement for the capacity values.

updatedOn.description: The UTC timestamp when the capacity data was last calculated.

AddonInfo.description: Information about an add-on allocation for the environment.

addonType.description: The type of add-on. AppPass is per-app passes, PerFlowPlan is per-flow licenses, PortalViews/PortalLogins are Power Pages entitlements, AI is AI Builder credits, PAUnattendedRPA is unattended RPA add-ons.

allocated.description: The number of allocated add-on units.

addonUnit.description: The unit of measurement for the add-on.

LinkedEnvironmentMetadata.description: Metadata about the linked Dataverse (Common Data Service) instance associated with the environment. Null if the environment does not have a Dataverse database.

resourceId.description: The resource identifier of the Dataverse instance.

friendlyName.description: The friendly display name of the Dataverse instance.

uniqueName.description: The unique name identifier for the Dataverse organization. Used in URL construction.

domainName.description: The domain name prefix used to construct the Dataverse instance URL.

instanceUrl.description: The primary URL of the Dataverse instance (used for web client access).

instanceApiUrl.description: The API endpoint URL for the Dataverse instance (used for Web API calls).

baseLanguage.description: The LCID (Locale Identifier) of the base language for the Dataverse instance. 1033 is English (United States).

instanceState.description: The operational state of the Dataverse instance.

createdTime.description: The UTC timestamp when the Dataverse instance was created.

backgroundOperationsState.description: Whether background operations (async processing, workflows) are enabled.

scaleGroup.description: The scale group identifier within the Dataverse infrastructure.

platformSku.description: The platform SKU tier of the Dataverse instance.
